element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Space exploration themed mechanics with planets and launch sequences

element_introduction_order.element: Steel Wheel table (steam train/Old West theme)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Train-themed ramps and track-based ball routing
Charts, bands, and tour progression mechanics; noted as having advanced features
Graveyard and demon-themed hazards; layout and rules based on Terminator 2 pinball machine; most complex table
Core mechanic constraining all table elements to physically realizable designs
Standard pinball mechanics present across all four tables

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade-driven tension cycles dependent on ball physics and player control; Nightmare table escalates tension through thematic hazard density

difficulty_curve.early_game: Ignition and Steel Wheel tables present foundational pinball mechanics with moderate complexity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Beat Box introduces advanced feature progression (charts, bands, tours); Amiga 1200 version has non-functional advanced features due to known bug
difficulty_curve.late_game: Nightmare table presents highest complexity with graveyard hazards and demon-themed mechanics based on T2 pinball design; red blood elements changed to blue in SNES port (censorship)
notable_spikes: Transition to Nightmare table from earlier tables
Beat Box advanced features unlock (where functional)

notable_level_patterns.description: Four distinct pinball tables each with coherent theme (space, western, music, horror) and corresponding mechanical design language
All table elements restricted to physically realizable components (no supernatural mechanics), creating unified design language across all tables
Nightmare table layout and rules directly based on Terminator 2: Judgment Day pinball machine released 1991

sources_used: wikipedia - Pinball Dreams main article (gameplay section, development details)
tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor Pinball Dreams (SNES) article (regional differences, port notes)
